majorProjects.title: Africa Long Life Study (ALLS)
majorProjects.description: A groundbreaking longitudinal study exploring psychological development of young adults in Namibia, Kenya, and South Africa, integrating etic (imported) with emic (Africentric) approaches to test global findings while building local theory.
majorProjects.significance: Addresses the critical gap in psychological science where most research comes from Western industrialized settings (only 11% of world population)

funding.description: Pan-African Longitudinal Study: Renewing Psychology with Strong Tests of Universality and New Insights
Core funding for longitudinal research infrastructure

objectives: Test replicability of Western psychological findings in African contexts
Build theory about local psychological phenomena and topics of interest
Generate high-quality data for global psychological science
Provide data for local mental health efforts
Mentor students and build research capacity in Africa

impact.scientific: First major longitudinal study spanning multiple African countries
impact.community: Provides data for local mental health interventions
impact.capacity: Mentoring next generation of African researchers

publications.title: Lifespan research in Kenya, Namibia, and South Africa: Cohort profile of the Africa long life study
publications.authors: Thalmayer, A. G., Asatsa, S., Shino, E. N., Naudé, L., Laher, S., et al.

majorProjects.title: Traditional Luhya Mourning Rituals: A Cultural Evolutionary Approach
majorProjects.description: Research examining how traditional Luhya mourning rituals contribute to individual wellbeing, community cooperation, and cultural transmission in the face of modernization and Christianization.
majorProjects.significance: Selected as one of 16 global projects funded by Templeton Foundation through Cultural Evolution Society

funding.description: Major grant for research on traditional Luhya mourning rituals and cultural transformation
Grant for scholar mobility and collaborative research activities
Recognition for outstanding international research collaboration

objectives: Explore therapeutic value of Luhya traditional mourning rituals
Examine role of mourning rituals on community cooperation
Evaluate role of mourning rituals on cultural transmission
Investigate impact of globalization on traditional practices

impact.cultural: Documenting and preserving indigenous knowledge systems
impact.community: Understanding mental health benefits of traditional practices
impact.policy: Informing culturally appropriate mental health interventions

methodology.design: Quantitative ethnography design with anthropological techniques
methodology.participants: Luhya community members and Christian leaders

majorProjects.title: COVID-19 Impacts on Adolescent Emotional Regulation
majorProjects.description: International research examining how the COVID-19 pandemic affected emotional regulation and mental health among adolescents across different cultural contexts.

funding.type: COVID-19 Network Early Career Scholars Fellowship
funding.description: Fellowship for research on COVID-19 impacts on adolescent development

funding.organization: International Consortium of Developmental Science Societies
funding.description: Recognition for research on COVID-19 impacts on adolescent development

collaborators.organization: International Consortium of Developmental Science Societies

findings: Significant variation in coping strategies across cultures
Importance of community support systems during pandemic
Gender differences in emotional regulation responses
Impact of school closures on adolescent mental health

emergingProjects.title: Decolonizing Psychology Curriculum in African Universities
emergingProjects.description: Developing and implementing psychology curricula that integrate indigenous knowledge systems and African perspectives.
emergingProjects.focus: Education reform, indigenous knowledge, curriculum development

emergingProjects.title: Culturally Validated Personality Assessment for African Contexts
emergingProjects.description: Creating and validating personality assessment tools that are culturally appropriate for African populations.
